title: zosterophyllanthos horichii
intro: Zosterophyllanthos horichii, also known as the Christmas Island staff vine, is a flowering plant species endemic to Christmas Island, a territory of Australia. It belongs to the family Celastraceae and is a type of woody climber. The plant has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. In traditional medicine, Zosterophyllanthos horichii is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues.
